entry.title: Atlas M0
entry.description: Atlas M0 provides a free, managed MongoDB cluster for learning, prototyping, and small applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free forever tier for a single small MongoDB cluster.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a MongoDB Atlas account.
Create a new project and select 'Build a Database'.
Choose the free M0 cluster tier and preferred cloud provider/region.
Configure cluster settings and deploy.
Add a database user and IP whitelist.
Connect to your cluster using the provided connection string.
entry.whenToUse: Use Atlas M0 for learning MongoDB, prototyping, or running small, low-traffic apps without production requirements.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Atlas M0 for production workloads, high-traffic apps, or when you need backups, higher performance, or advanced features.
entry.body: Atlas M0 is MongoDB's always-free managed cluster, ideal for learning, prototyping, and small projects with strict resource and feature limits.

entry.title: Atlassian Jira Free
entry.description: Jira Free offers cloud-based project and issue tracking for small teams with essential agile management features.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free for up to 10 users with core agile features and basic project management tools.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Atlassian account.
Create a new Jira Software site.
Invite up to 9 team members.
Set up your first project and board.
Start creating and assigning issues.
entry.whenToUse: Use Jira Free for small teams needing agile boards, issue tracking, and basic project management without advanced admin controls.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need more than 10 users, advanced permissions, or enterprise-level reporting and integrations.
entry.body: Atlassian Jira Free provides essential agile project management and issue tracking for small teams, ideal for lightweight workflows and early-stage projects.

entry.title: Azure Cosmos DB
entry.description: Globally distributed, multi-model NoSQL database service with low latency and automatic scaling.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 400 RU/s provisioned throughput and 5 GB storage per month.
caveats: Free tier must be selected at account creation

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal.
Create a new Cosmos DB account and select the free tier option.
Choose your preferred API (Core SQL, MongoDB, Cassandra, Gremlin, Table).
Configure region, throughput, and other settings.
Create a database and container.
Connect your application using the provided connection string.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a globally distributed NoSQL database with low latency and automatic scaling for diverse workloads.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require a traditional relational database or need a free tier without requiring a credit card.
entry.body: Azure Cosmos DB offers a globally distributed, multi-model NoSQL database with automatic scaling, low latency, and a generous free tier for new workloads.

entry.title: Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server
entry.description: Managed PostgreSQL database service with flexible configuration, scaling, and high availability on Azure.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes one burstable B1MS instance with 32 GB storage per month.
caveats: No high availability in free tier
Limited to one free instance per subscription
billingRiskNotes: Charges apply if you exceed instance or storage limits

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal.
Navigate to 'Create a resource' and select 'Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server'.
Choose the 'Burstable B1MS' compute tier and configure storage up to 32 GB.
Select a supported region and complete required settings.
Review and create the server.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a managed PostgreSQL database for development, testing, or small production workloads with minimal setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need high availability, large-scale production, or require regions not supported by the free tier.
entry.body: Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server provides a managed, scalable PostgreSQL database with a free tier suitable for development, learning, and small workloads. The free tier offers a single burstable B1MS instance with 32 GB storage, making it ideal for prototyping and testing. However, it lacks high availability and is only available in select regions, so it's not suitable for large-scale or mission-critical production deployments.

entry.title: Azure SQL Database
entry.description: Managed relational database service for SQL Server workloads with built-in high availability and scalability.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier offers 250GB storage with limited performance on the Basic service tier.
caveats: Performance is limited compared to paid tiers

useCases: Developing and testing SQL Server apps
Learning SQL Server in the cloud
quickstartSteps: Sign in to the Azure Portal.
Navigate to 'Create a resource' and select 'SQL Database'.
Choose the free tier (Basic, 250GB) and configure database settings.
Create a new SQL server or select an existing one.
Review and create the database.
Connect using Azure Data Studio or SQL Server Management Studio.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a managed SQL Server database for development, learning, or small production workloads with minimal setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced performance, features, or need to scale beyond the free tier's storage and compute limits.
entry.body: Azure SQL Database provides a fully managed, scalable SQL Server database with a free tier suitable for development, learning, and small workloads. The free tier is limited to 250GB storage and basic performance, making it ideal for prototyping and educational use but not for high-performance or large-scale production applications.

entry.title: CockroachDB Cloud
entry.description: Managed distributed SQL database service with PostgreSQL compatibility and global scalability.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes a single cluster with up to 5GB storage and 1 vCPU.
caveats: No production SLA on free tier

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a CockroachDB Cloud account.
Create a new free cluster in your preferred cloud region.
Download connection credentials and certificates.
Connect using a PostgreSQL-compatible client.
Create tables and start loading data.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a managed, scalable SQL database with PostgreSQL compatibility for prototyping or small workloads.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid for high-throughput production workloads or if you require advanced support and SLAs.
entry.body: CockroachDB Cloud offers a free managed distributed SQL database cluster with PostgreSQL compatibility, ideal for learning, prototyping, and testing global data distribution.

entry.title: DynamoDB
entry.description: DynamoDB is a fully managed NoSQL database service by AWS for fast and flexible performance at scale.

freeTierDetails.summary: Always-free tier includes 25 GB storage and up to 200 million requests per month.
caveats: Limits apply per month and per AWS account.
billingRiskNotes: Usage above free tier is billed automatically.
Monitor usage to avoid unexpected charges.

quickstartSteps: Sign in to the AWS Management Console.
Navigate to DynamoDB and click 'Create table'.
Configure table name and primary key settings.
Choose default capacity mode (on-demand or provisioned).
Create the table and start adding items.
entry.whenToUse: Use DynamoDB for applications needing scalable, low-latency NoSQL storage with minimal operational overhead.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require complex relational queries, multi-table joins, or strong ACID transactions across tables.
entry.body: DynamoDB is AWS's fully managed NoSQL database, offering seamless scaling, high availability, and a generous always-free tier for new and existing users.

entry.title: EverSQL
entry.description: EverSQL is an online service that automatically optimizes SQL queries and provides indexing recommendations.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier allows limited SQL query optimization and index recommendations.
limits: Up to 5 query optimizations per month
No access to advanced performance analysis

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free EverSQL account.
Log in and navigate to the query optimization tool.
Paste your SQL query into the editor.
Click 'Optimize' to receive recommendations.
Review suggested query changes and indexes.
entry.whenToUse: Use EverSQL when you need quick, automated SQL query optimization and indexing advice without deep database expertise.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require unlimited optimizations, advanced tuning, or support for non-SQL databases.
entry.body: EverSQL helps developers and teams optimize SQL queries and indexes quickly, making it easier to improve database performance without manual tuning.

entry.title: Firebase Firestore
entry.description: A scalable, serverless NoSQL cloud database for building web and mobile apps with real-time data synchronization.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free Spark plan includes limited reads, writes, and storage per month.
caveats: Free tier is for non-commercial use only.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Firebase account and create a new project.
Navigate to Firestore in the Firebase console and enable it.
Choose test mode for development (locked mode for production).
Install Firebase SDK in your app and initialize Firestore.
Start reading and writing data using the Firestore client libraries.
entry.whenToUse: Use Firestore for real-time, serverless NoSQL databases in web or mobile apps with moderate scale and simple security needs.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need complex queries, strict relational data, or require on-premises hosting and advanced enterprise controls.
entry.body: Firebase Firestore is a fully managed, serverless NoSQL document database with real-time sync, ideal for rapid development of web and mobile applications.

entry.title: Firebase Realtime Database
entry.description: A cloud-hosted NoSQL database for building real-time, collaborative applications with automatic data synchronization.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free Spark plan with limited simultaneous connections and storage.
caveats: No custom domain support on free tier

quickstartSteps: Create a Firebase project at the Firebase Console.
Add Firebase Realtime Database to your project.
Configure database rules for access control.
Integrate Firebase SDK into your app.
Read and write data using provided SDK methods.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need real-time data sync across clients with minimal backend setup, especially for mobile or collaborative apps.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need complex queries, multi-region replication, or strong relational data consistency.
entry.body: Firebase Realtime Database offers a free tier suitable for small-scale real-time apps, with easy integration and automatic data synchronization across devices.

entry.title: MongoDB Atlas
entry.description: Fully managed MongoDB database service with free tier hosting on AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free shared cluster (M0) with 512 MB storage, basic features, and limited performance.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a MongoDB Atlas account.
Create a new project and build a free M0 cluster.
Choose your preferred cloud provider and region.
Configure database access and network rules.
Connect your application using the provided connection string.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a managed NoSQL database for prototyping, learning, or small-scale production with minimal setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require high performance, large storage, dedicated clusters, or advanced features like backups and custom VPC peering.
entry.body: MongoDB Atlas offers a free, fully managed MongoDB cluster suitable for learning, prototyping, and small applications, with easy cloud deployment and basic features.

entry.title: Neon
entry.description: Neon is a serverless Postgres platform with autoscaling, branching, and a generous free tier.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 10 GB storage, 3 branches, and 1 project with 500,000 Postgres compute seconds per month.
caveats: Compute pauses after 5 minutes of inactivity
No point-in-time recovery on free tier

quickstartSteps: Sign up at neon.tech using your email or GitHub account.
Create a new project and select your region.
Configure your database and create a branch if needed.
Copy the connection string and use it in your application.
Monitor usage and manage branches from the dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use Neon when you need a managed Postgres database with easy branching and a generous free tier for development or small projects.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Neon if you require advanced Postgres extensions, high sustained throughput, or strict uptime SLAs for production workloads.
entry.body: Neon offers a serverless Postgres experience with branching and autoscaling, making it ideal for development, prototyping, and open source projects with a generous always-free tier.

entry.title: Pinecone
entry.description: Pinecone is a managed vector database for building fast, scalable similarity search and AI applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes one pod with up to 1GB storage and limited queries.
caveats: Limited performance and features compared to paid plans

useCases: Similarity search for images or text
quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Pinecone account at pinecone.io.
Create a new project and select the Starter environment.
Set up your first index with desired dimensions and metric.
Install the Pinecone client SDK in your application.
Insert vectors and perform similarity queries using the SDK.
entry.whenToUse: Use Pinecone when you need fast, scalable vector search for AI or similarity-based applications without managing infrastructure.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced features, high throughput, or production SLAs on the free tier, or need on-premise deployment.
entry.body: Pinecone provides a fully managed vector database service, enabling developers to build and scale AI-powered search and recommendation systems with minimal setup and maintenance.

entry.title: PlanetScale
entry.description: PlanetScale is a serverless MySQL-compatible database platform built on Vitess for scalable, production-grade applications.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 5GB storage, 1 production branch, and up to 100 million row reads/month.
caveats: No support for direct database connections (only through PlanetScale-provided endpoints)
No backups or point-in-time recovery on free tier

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free PlanetScale account.
Create a new database from the dashboard.
Create a development branch and promote it to production.
Connect your app using the provided connection string.
Start reading and writing data.
entry.whenToUse: Use PlanetScale when you need a scalable, serverless MySQL-compatible database with zero-downtime branching and schema changes.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced MySQL features like triggers, stored procedures, or need direct database access or custom backup options.
entry.body: PlanetScale offers a serverless, scalable MySQL-compatible database with a generous free tier, ideal for modern app development and prototyping. Its branching and deploy workflow enables safe schema changes, but advanced MySQL features and direct DB access are limited on the free plan.

entry.title: Qdrant
entry.description: Qdrant is a vector database service for storing, searching, and managing high-dimensional embeddings at scale.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 1GB storage and up to 10,000 requests per month.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Qdrant Cloud account.
Create a new project and collection in the dashboard.
Obtain your API key from the project settings.
Connect using the Qdrant client library in your preferred language.
Upload your vector data and start querying.
entry.whenToUse: Use Qdrant when you need to store and search high-dimensional vectors for AI or similarity-based applications.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Qdrant if you require traditional relational queries or need to store large datasets beyond the free tier limits.
entry.body: Qdrant offers a managed vector database service with a generous free tier, ideal for prototyping AI-powered search and similarity applications.

entry.title: Redis
entry.description: Redis is an in-memory data store used for caching, real-time analytics, and fast key-value storage.

freeTierDetails.summary: Redis Cloud offers a free tier with limited memory and throughput.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Redis Cloud account at redis.com.
Create a new free database instance.
Copy the connection string from the dashboard.
Install a Redis client library in your application.
Connect to your Redis instance using the provided credentials.
entry.whenToUse: Use Redis when you need fast, in-memory data storage for caching, session management, or real-time analytics.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Redis for persistent, relational data or if you require strong durability guarantees.
entry.body: Redis is a popular in-memory data store ideal for caching, session storage, and real-time analytics, with a free cloud tier suitable for development and small projects.

entry.title: Redis Cloud
entry.description: Fully managed Redis service offering scalable, high-performance in-memory data storage and caching.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes a single database with up to 30MB storage.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Redis Cloud account.
Create a new free database instance.
Select your preferred cloud provider and region.
Copy the database endpoint and credentials.
Connect from your application using a Redis client.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a managed, low-latency cache or in-memory database for small-scale apps or prototyping.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require high availability, persistence, or large datasets beyond 30MB in production.
entry.body: Redis Cloud offers a free, fully managed Redis database suitable for caching, prototyping, and learning, with a 30MB storage limit and no persistence.

entry.title: Sqlable
entry.description: Sqlable provides a serverless SQL API for querying CSV, Excel, and JSON files directly from cloud storage.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier allows limited SQL queries on files from cloud storage.
caveats: Limited to public cloud storage files

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Sqlable account.
Connect your cloud storage provider (e.g., AWS S3, Google Drive).
Upload or select a CSV, Excel, or JSON file.
Write and execute SQL queries using the web UI or API.
Review query results and export if needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use Sqlable when you need to run SQL queries on flat files stored in cloud storage without setting up a database.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require persistent relational databases, high query volumes, or advanced SQL features beyond basic file querying.
entry.body: Sqlable is a serverless SQL API that enables users to query CSV, Excel, and JSON files directly from cloud storage, ideal for quick data analysis and prototyping without database setup.

entry.title: Supabase Database
entry.description: A hosted PostgreSQL database with instant APIs, authentication, and real-time features for modern app development.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes a managed PostgreSQL database with limited storage and compute.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Supabase account.
Create a new project and choose a database password.
Access the database credentials and connection string from the dashboard.
Use the SQL editor or connect via your preferred PostgreSQL client.
Integrate Supabase client libraries into your app for authentication and realtime features.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a quick, managed PostgreSQL backend with built-in auth and realtime for prototypes or small projects.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid for high-traffic production workloads or when you need advanced PostgreSQL extensions or custom configurations.
entry.body: Supabase Database offers a managed PostgreSQL instance with instant APIs, authentication, and realtime features, ideal for rapid prototyping and small-scale apps.

entry.title: Upstash Redis
entry.description: Serverless Redis database with per-request pricing and a generous free tier for low-latency caching and data storage.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes 10,000 requests/day, 256 MB storage, and 1 database.
caveats: No custom VPC support on free tier

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Upstash account.
Create a new Redis database from the dashboard.
Copy the provided endpoint and credentials.
Integrate the Redis endpoint into your application using a Redis client.
Monitor usage and metrics in the Upstash dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need a simple, serverless Redis database for caching, session storage, or lightweight data needs with minimal setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced Redis features, high throughput, custom VPC, or multi-region replication.
entry.body: Upstash Redis offers a serverless, low-maintenance Redis database with a generous free tier, ideal for developers needing fast, scalable caching or key-value storage without managing infrastructure.
